name: Linsoles Reservoir Loop Trail
name_original: Embalse de Linsoles – trail running desde Eriste
description: Enjoy an easy 2.5-mile (4.1 km) jogging route around the scenic Linsoles Reservoir, perfect for a quick and relaxing run.
region_intro: Hiking around Sahún offers access to the Benasque Valley within the Aragonese Pyrenees, characterized by high peaks, glacial lakes, and verdant valleys. The region is home to the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park and the Natural Monument of the Pyrenean Glaciers. Trails often traverse the left bank of the River Esera and lead through diverse terrain, from dense forests to open grasslands. Best hiking trails around Sahún The most popular hiking route is From Cascada de Espigantosa to Tuca de Posets - Posets-Maladeta Natural Park, a 10.2 miles (16.4 km) trail that takes 10 hours 37 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends to Tuca de Posets, one of the highest peaks in the Pyrenees. Another top favourite among local hikers is Espigantosa Waterfall via Traviesa and return via Tramarius - Posets-Maladeta Natural Park, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path. This trail features the Cascada de Espigantosa and traverses varied valley landscapes. Local hikers also love the Lake of Eriste — round trip to the Dam, a 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail leading through gentle terrain to the Lake of Eriste, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Hiking around Sahún is defined by high peaks, pristine glacial lakes (ibones), and spectacular waterfalls within verdant valleys. The network offers options for easy strolls to demanding high-altitude expeditions. The routes in Sahún are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 370 reviews. More than 3600 hikers have used komoot to explore Sahún's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Sahún?
A: Sahún offers a diverse network of over 50 hiking trails, catering to various fitness levels. These routes explore the stunning landscapes of the Benasque Valley and the Aragonese Pyrenees.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families in Sahún?
A: Yes, Sahún has several easy trails perfect for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1193748344/lake-of-eriste-round-trip-to-the-dam" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of Eriste — round trip to the Dam&lt;/a&gt; is a gentle 2.6-mile (4.2 km) path, often completed in just over an hour, leading to the serene Lake of Eriste.

Q: Can I find challenging multi-day hikes in the Sahún area?
A: Absolutely. The region is ideal for demanding high-altitude expeditions. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1225020876/from-cascada-de-espigantosa-to-tuca-de-posets-posets-maladeta-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Cascada de Espigantosa to Tuca de Posets&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and lead to major peaks. The area also hosts sections of long-distance routes like the GR-11 Transpirenaica, allowing for multi-day treks with mountain refuges like Refugio Angel Orus as rest stops.

Q: What natural attractions, like waterfalls or lakes, can I see on hikes near Sahún?
A: Sahún's trails are rich with natural beauty. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1194736407/espigantosa-waterfall-via-traviesa-and-return-via-tramarius-posets-maladeta-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Espigantosa&lt;/a&gt;, a notable attraction and starting point for several routes. The region is also famous for its pristine glacial lakes, known as &lt;em&gt;ibones&lt;/em&gt;, with popular routes leading to the Ibones of Barbarisa, the Lake of Eriste, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357105986/ibon-de-la-llardaneta-y-coll-de-grist-desde-la-espigantosa-parque-natural-posets-maladeta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón de la Llardaneta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Sahún and the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park?
A: While many trails in the broader Aragonese Pyrenees are dog-friendly, specific regulations apply within the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park to protect wildlife. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas, and to check local signage for any specific restrictions on certain routes or during particular seasons.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sahún?
A: The best time for hiking in Sahún is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally mild, and most trails are free of snow. High-altitude routes are best tackled in summer (July-August) when conditions are most stable. Winter offers opportunities for snowshoeing and ski mountaineering, but requires specialized equipment and experience.

Q: Do I need any permits to hike in the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park near Sahún?
A: For general hiking on marked trails within the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park, permits are typically not required. However, for specific activities like climbing certain peaks (e.g., Tuca de Posets) or accessing sensitive areas, it's always advisable to check with the park authorities for any current regulations or permit requirements, especially for large groups or organized events.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Sahún?
A: The hiking routes around Sahún are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 370 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning alpine scenery, the variety of trails from easy strolls to challenging ascents, and the pristine nature of the glacial lakes and waterfalls.

Q: Are there mountain refuges available for hikers in the Sahún region?
A: Yes, the Sahún region, particularly within the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park, has several mountain refuges that provide essential rest stops and accommodation for hikers. Notable examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1205885947/refugio-angel-orus-from-cascada-de-espigantosa-parque-natural-posets-maladeta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Angel Orus&lt;/a&gt; and Refugio del Forcau, which are often starting or ending points for more demanding high-altitude routes.

Q: How can I reach the trailheads around Sahún using public transport?
A: While Sahún itself is a charming village, public transport options to specific trailheads within the Benasque Valley can be limited, especially for more remote routes. It's often best to check local bus schedules from larger towns in the Benasque Valley, such as Benasque, which may offer seasonal services to popular starting points like the Cascada de Espigantosa. For ultimate flexibility, a car is recommended.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, Sahún offers cultural insights. The village itself boasts traditional high-mountain architecture, an old bridge, and the Romanesque-Lombard hermitage of San Aventín. Traditional paths connect Sahún to other villages in the Benasque Valley, providing opportunities for gentler walks that combine nature with local heritage.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Sahún?
A: Hiking around Sahún offers incredibly diverse landscapes. You'll traverse lush valleys like the Benasque Valley, dense forests, and open grasslands along the River Esera. Higher up, expect dramatic alpine scenery with majestic peaks, including the Tuca de Posets, pristine glacial lakes (ibones), and spectacular waterfalls. The region is part of the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park, ensuring breathtaking views at every turn.
tips: The Linsoles (or Eriste) Reservoir was built in the 1960s to collect the water channeled through the mountain to power the Eriste hydroelectric power station. It is one of the 3 reservoirs of the Ésera river in the Benasque valley.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered an easy run, making it ideal for beginners, casual joggers, and families. Its relatively flat terrain and gentle nature mean it doesn't require significant effort, perfect for a relaxed outing.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain around the Linsoles Reservoir primarily features mostly paved or gravel surfaces, with some asphalt sections. While parts of the trail are tracks or roads, it also includes pleasant paths and streets. There's a beautiful path leading down to Eriste that can be somewhat stony but is very enjoyable.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy picturesque views of the Linsoles Reservoir itself, which collects water from the Ésera river. The surrounding landscape includes impressive mountains, and you might pass through pleasant mixed forests. You'll also cross the Ésera River Bridge in Eriste.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer mornings or late afternoons are also great to avoid the midday heat. The serene atmosphere makes it a good choice in any season.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is easily accessible from Eriste. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Eriste serves as a common starting point, suggesting parking is available within the village or near the reservoir access points.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, most trails in this region are dog-friendly. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially around populated areas or if there are livestock nearby, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to jog this route around the Embalse de Linsoles. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. It covers 100% of the "La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 22. Eriste - Sesué - San Sadurní" and "PR-HU 51 Pista" trails. It also shares sections with "Eriste – Casa de Conques – Anciles" (37%), "La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 24. Benasque – Conques – Presa Linsoles – Benasque" (37%), "Benasque – Ermita de San José – Eriste" (27%), and "Sendero Praus de Cases" (27%).

faqs.question: Is it a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The primary route around the Linsoles Reservoir is a very pleasant and easy circuit, making it a loop trail. This is ideal for those seeking a relaxed run or active recovery.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jog on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this easy jogging route, comfortable running shoes, appropriate athletic wear for the weather, and a water bottle are usually sufficient. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is advisable on sunny days. If you plan to extend your run beyond the reservoir, consider bringing snacks.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to rest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route around the Linsoles Reservoir is known for having benches and tables along the way, providing convenient opportunities for rest and enjoying the scenic views.
